title: Observer must not wipe the run's circuit-breaker cooldown
description: adw/orchestrator.py run_observer wrote a fresh State (cooldown_until=None) to the run's state.json, overwriting the cooldown the breaker had just set on a halt (evidence: S-017's state.json showed cooldown_until=null despite the circuit having opened). That lets an auto-retry start before the cooldown elapses, defeating the breaker's pause for every non-quota halt (the quotad path already skips the observer). Fix: run_observer loads the existing state.json and carries cooldown_until forward when it belongs to the same ticket, preserving the pause while still threading last_failure into the observe prompt.
acceptance_criteria: After a halt that set cooldown_until, running the observer leaves cooldown_until intact in state.json while still presenting the failure reason to the observe stage
check_cooldown still reports an active cooldown after the observer has run on a non-quota halt
A leftover state.json for a different ticket does not leak its cooldown onto the observe run
Unit tests cover: cooldown survives an observer run, observer still receives last_failure, and a different-ticket cooldown is ignored; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
